agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA, premium pricing, and conditional refund policy. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes across five stakeholder groups (Product, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, Legal) with significant unresolved contradictions.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of the broader Delivery/Fulfillment context, touching Order Placement, Driver Assignment, Real-Time Tracking, and Refund Processing",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse" },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge on top of the base delivery fee, charged when a customer selects Express Delivery" },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge, which is NOT refundable even when the express window is missed" },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period starting from order placement within which the express delivery is targeted to arrive. DISPUTED: whether this is a guarantee or best-effort SLA" },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute internal operational target for a driver to pick up an express order from the warehouse after placement" },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of at least 4.0 stars who is qualified to fulfill express deliveries" },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Customer", "definition": "A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse" },
      { "term": "Missed Window Refund", "definition": "A refund of the Express Fee (not the base fee) when the delivery window is not met, subject to exclusions. DISPUTED: whether this is automatic or a goodwill gesture" },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation Replacement Window", "definition": "A 10-minute period after a driver cancels mid-delivery during which a replacement driver must be assigned, or an automatic refund is triggered" },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion that applies when the customer provided an incorrect delivery address" }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical/logistical constraint — a 2-hour delivery is impossible beyond this radius given current operations. Violating this would make delivery windows unachievable.",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN a customer with a delivery address >10km from all warehouses, WHEN they attempt to select Express Delivery, THEN the option must not be available"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational target that protects the 2-hour window but is a tunable policy threshold, not a data integrity constraint",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN an express order placed at time T, WHEN no driver has picked it up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (alerting, reassignment, or other operational response)"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating >= 4.0 stars may be assigned to express deliveries",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality policy that could be adjusted based on driver supply; a VP could lower this to 3.5 if driver pool is insufficient",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN a driver with a rating of 3.9 or below, WHEN the system considers them for an express order, THEN they must be excluded from assignment"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can change with market conditions",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN a customer selecting Express Delivery, WHEN the order total is calculated, THEN the Express Fee of $12.99 must be added to the base delivery fee"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the delivery window is missed, only the Express Fee is refunded — the base delivery fee is never refunded for a missed window",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Financial integrity constraint — the base fee covers the cost of actual delivery performed. Refunding it would mean the company pays to deliver for free.",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN a missed express delivery window triggering a refund, WHEN the refund is processed, THEN the refund amount must equal the Express Fee only, not include the base delivery fee"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No missed-window refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Causality/fairness constraint — the company cannot be held responsible for delays caused by customer error. This protects against fraud and moral hazard.",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN an express delivery that missed its window AND the delivery address was flagged as incorrect by the driver, WHEN a refund is evaluated, THEN the refund must be denied"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund of the Express Fee is triggered",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational SLA that could be adjusted; the 10-minute threshold and auto-refund are policy choices",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN an express delivery where the assigned driver cancels, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned, THEN an automatic refund of the Express Fee must be initiated"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express deliveries must provide real-time driver tracking to the customer",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint — delivery can still complete without tracking",
      "testable_assertion": "GIVEN an active express delivery, WHEN the customer views the order, THEN the driver's real-time location must be displayed"
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from a warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Must define boundary behavior. Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid customer frustration at edge." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers available at order time", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the system hide the express option, show it as unavailable, or allow the order and hope a driver becomes available within 15min? This is a critical gap." },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up order at minute 14 but delivery takes 2+ hours total", "expected_behavior": "Pickup SLA was met but delivery window was missed. Refund should still apply per BR-5, but Operations may dispute since they met their internal target." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after placing an express order", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does address change void the express SLA? Does the new address need to be within 10km? Is this treated as a 'wrong address' for refund exclusion purposes?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels after the first driver cancellation", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 10-minute replacement window reset, or is the auto-refund already triggered? What is the maximum number of reassignment attempts?" },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:55 PM", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — are there operating hours for express delivery? A 2-hour window crossing midnight may have no available drivers." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km; nearest is out of stock but a farther one has inventory", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — which warehouse fulfills the order? Distance is measured to 'a warehouse' but doesn't specify which. Fulfillment from a farther warehouse impacts the delivery window." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window", "expected_behavior": "The 10-minute replacement window extends past the delivery window. The auto-refund should trigger at minute 120 (window miss), not minute 125 (replacement window expiry)." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides correct address but is unreachable/gate code doesn't work", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is this a 'wrong address' exclusion? Address was correct but delivery could not be completed due to access issues. Need clear definition of 'wrong address'." },
    { "scenario": "Missed-window refund for an order where Finance's 3-business-day processing conflicts with Customer Success's 'automatic refund'", "expected_behavior": "CONTRADICTION — see escalation E-2. Must resolve whether refunds are instant credits or processed within 3 business days." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee at $12.99 represents a high-margin revenue stream. However, refund policy ambiguity (automatic vs. 3-day processing) creates unpredictable financial exposure. If the 'guarantee' language is used in marketing and Legal's concern is ignored, the company faces potential liability for false advertising or class-action risk on unfulfilled guarantees.",
    "operational": "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the eligible driver pool. During peak hours or in areas with few drivers, this may make express delivery undeliverable — creating a poor customer experience when the option is visible but no drivers can fulfill it. The 10-minute replacement window for driver cancellations adds further pressure on dispatch systems.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and a prominent 2-hour window are strong value propositions. However, if the product is marketed as a 'guarantee' but the refund is actually a discretionary goodwill gesture, customer trust will erode rapidly after the first missed window where a refund is denied. The 'wrong address' exclusion is also ripe for customer disputes.",
    "risk": "CRITICAL: The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction (E-1) is a legal and reputational landmine. Launching with 'guaranteed' language while Legal insists it's best-effort could result in regulatory action, especially in jurisdictions with consumer protection laws around delivery promises. This must be resolved before any code is written.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Primary beneficiaries of faster delivery; at risk of confusion around guarantee language and refund eligibility" },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star rating filter reduces eligible pool; express orders create time pressure that may affect driver satisfaction and safety" },
      { "group": "Operations/Dispatch", "impact": "Must build real-time monitoring for 15-min pickup SLA and 10-min replacement window; significant system investment" },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Must handle new refund workflows; exposure to refund volume if SLAs are frequently missed" },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must approve all customer-facing language; 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' has direct regulatory implications" },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes on 'wrong address' exclusions and missed window complaints; needs clear escalation policies" }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is very low because the spec contains a fundamental contradiction between Product/Customer Success ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort, not a guarantee') that affects the entire product's positioning, refund policy, and customer-facing language. Additionally, there is a second contradiction between automatic refunds and 3-business-day processing. Multiple critical gaps (driver availability, operating hours, warehouse selection, address change handling) remain unaddressed. This spec is not ready for implementation.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "10km radius eligibility constraint is clear and uncontested",
      "Express Fee amount of $12.99 is agreed upon",
      "Base delivery fee is not refundable — all parties agree",
      "4-star driver rating minimum is stated by Operations",
      "Real-time tracking is a required feature"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA",
      "Whether missed-window refunds are automatic or require 3-business-day processing",
      "What exactly constitutes a 'wrong address' for refund exclusion purposes",
      "How the system behaves when no express-eligible drivers are available",
      "Operating hours and geographic edge cases",
      "How the delivery window clock interacts with the replacement driver window"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (haversine) distance, not driving distance — this is not specified",
      "The 2-hour delivery window starts from order placement, not from driver pickup — inferred from Operations note but not explicitly stated",
      "Driver rating of 4.0 stars refers to an aggregate/lifetime rating, not a recent-period rating — not specified",
      "The 'wrong address' exclusion requires driver confirmation/evidence, not just a system flag — process is undefined"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "CRITICAL: Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee,' while Legal explicitly states it is a 'best effort' SLA and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This is not a nuance — it is a fundamental contradiction that affects product positioning, marketing language, refund policy enforceability, and regulatory compliance. The entire product cannot be built until this is resolved.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Marketing", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Market as 'guarantee' with automatic refunds (PM's vision) — Legal must approve or explicitly accept the risk",
        "B: Market as 'express target' or 'aim to deliver within 2 hours' with goodwill refunds at company discretion (Legal's position)",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'guarantee' with an asterisk/terms-of-service carveout that limits legal exposure while preserving the customer promise"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C is the industry standard (e.g., Domino's '30 minutes or free' evolved this way), but Legal MUST sign off on exact language before development begins. This is a blocker."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refunds for driver cancellation without replacement should be 'automatic,' but Finance says all missed-window refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These are incompatible — an automatic refund is instant (or near-instant as a credit), while a 3-business-day process implies manual review or batch processing.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: All express refunds are instant account credits (Customer Success's expectation) — Finance adjusts their process",
        "B: All express refunds go through 3-business-day processing (Finance's position) — Customer Success adjusts expectations",
        "C: Driver-cancellation refunds are automatic instant credits; missed-window refunds are processed in 3 business days (split policy)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C balances both concerns — driver cancellation is clearly the company's fault and warrants instant goodwill, while missed-window refunds may require investigation (e.g., wrong address check) justifying a processing period. But both teams must agree."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "'Wrong address' is undefined. Does it mean: (a) address doesn't exist, (b) address exists but customer isn't there, (c) address is correct but inaccessible (gated community, locked building), (d) customer typo that driver corrected en route? Each interpretation changes refund eligibility dramatically. Customer Support will face disputes daily without a precise definition.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Drivers", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Narrow definition — only addresses that are provably non-existent or unreachable (driver GPS evidence required)",
        "B: Broad definition — any address issue reported by the driver, including access problems",
        "C: Tiered definition — non-existent address = no refund; access issues = partial refund or retry"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with driver photo/GPS evidence. Broad definitions invite driver abuse of the exclusion and customer disputes. Define clearly and train drivers on evidence collection."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when express delivery is selected but no express-eligible drivers (>=4 stars, within range) are currently available. This is likely the most common failure mode in early rollout and during peak hours.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations/Dispatch", "Product"],
      "options": [
        "A: Hide the Express option when no drivers are available (avoids broken promises but reduces conversion)",
        "B: Accept the order and attempt assignment, with a fallback to standard delivery + refund if 15-min pickup SLA is missed",
        "C: Show Express as available but with a warning ('express drivers are in high demand, delivery may take longer')"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A is safest for launch. Never offer what you can't deliver. Option B creates refund exposure and customer frustration."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The 2-hour delivery window start time is not explicitly defined. Does it start at order placement, order confirmation, payment confirmation, or driver assignment? A 15-minute pickup delay already consumes 12.5% of the window — the start time definition materially affects whether the SLA is achievable.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations/Dispatch", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Window starts at order placement (customer's mental model, but hardest to meet)",
        "B: Window starts at order confirmation/payment (slight buffer for payment processing)",
        "C: Window starts at driver pickup (easiest to meet operationally, but customer sees a longer wait)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — the customer's clock starts when they press 'Place Order.' Any other definition will feel dishonest and generate complaints. Operations must design around this constraint, not redefine it."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Spec contains a fundamental contradiction on whether the delivery window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA", "business_rule_broken": "Cannot define refund policy, customer-facing language, or acceptance criteria when the core product promise is disputed between Product and Legal" },
    { "violation": "Refund timing is contradictory — automatic vs. 3 business days", "business_rule_broken": "Cannot implement refund processing when Finance and Customer Success have incompatible expectations" }
  ]
}
